obligations.rule: The function must validate credit card numbers without any options (default behavior).
obligations.why: The README specifies that the function checks if a string is a credit card number, and the default behavior applies without options.

obligations.rule: The function must validate credit card numbers for specific providers when the 'provider' option is set.
obligations.why: The README specifies that the 'provider' option restricts validation to specific credit card issuers.

obligations.rule: The function must reject strings that are not numeric or contain invalid characters.
obligations.why: Credit card numbers are numeric strings, and invalid characters should cause rejection.

obligations.rule: The function must reject strings that are too short or too long to be valid credit card numbers.
obligations.why: Credit card numbers have specific length constraints depending on the provider.

obligations.rule: The function must reject strings that fail the Luhn algorithm check.
obligations.why: The Luhn algorithm is a standard checksum used to validate credit card numbers.

test_groups.title: Default behavior: validate general credit card numbers
test_groups.rationale: Tests the default behavior of validating general credit card numbers without any options.

test_groups.title: Provider-specific validation: 'amex'
test_groups.rationale: Tests validation restricted to American Express credit card numbers using the 'provider' option.

test_groups.title: Provider-specific validation: 'visa'
test_groups.rationale: Tests validation restricted to Visa credit card numbers using the 'provider' option.

test_groups.title: Invalid character rejection
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of strings containing invalid characters or non-numeric content.

test_groups.title: Length validation: too short or too long
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of strings that are too short or too long to be valid credit card numbers.

test_groups.title: Luhn algorithm validation
test_groups.rationale: Tests rejection of strings that fail the Luhn algorithm check.
